title: India and France Elevate Ties to 'Special Global Strategic Partnership' During Macron Visit
lede: French President Emmanuel Macron visited India in February 2026 to jointly inaugurate the India-France Year of Innovation and elevate bilateral relations to a 'Special Global Strategic Partnership,' marking a significant deepening of defence, economic and technological cooperation.

sections.context: President Macron's official visit to India from 17-19 February 2026 represents his fourth trip to the country and follows Prime Minister Modi's reciprocal visit to France in February 2025, demonstrating sustained high-level diplomatic engagement. The two leaders elevated the relationship from its existing Strategic Partnership to a 'Special Global Strategic Partnership,' a move framed around shared ambitions to position India and France as forces for global good in an increasingly uncertain international environment. This upgrade reflects accumulated progress since both countries celebrated the 25th anniversary of their Strategic Partnership in 2023, when they adopted the Horizon 2047 Roadmap—a long-term framework extending cooperation through 2047, which marks both the centenary of Indian independence and 50 years of bilateral strategic partnership. The timing coincides with the jointly inaugurated India-France Year of Innovation in Mumbai, underscoring technological collaboration as a cornerstone of renewed ambitions.
sections.threads: The elevation of the partnership framework connects to broader Indo-Pacific security realignments, where France has positioned itself as a key strategic partner through deepened [[defence-collaboration|defence and security cooperation]] involving co-design, co-development and co-production of defence platforms. The establishment of an annual Foreign Ministers Comprehensive Dialogue signals institutionalization of the bilateral relationship at the highest diplomatic level, mirroring coordination mechanisms adopted by India with other major powers. The joint statement's emphasis on addressing global challenges within a rules-based international order reflects convergence with India's stated foreign policy positions on multilateralism, even as both nations navigate economic security concerns and technological competition in an increasingly multipolar world.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor the implementation of the newly established annual Foreign Ministers Comprehensive Dialogue, which will review progress on the elevated partnership and the Horizon 2047 Roadmap—particularly in the identified priority areas of [[economic-security|economic security]], global issues, and people-to-people exchanges. The statement references ongoing collaboration in [[civil-nuclear-energy|civil nuclear energy]] through fruitful discussions on potential collaborations, suggesting announcements may follow on joint projects or technology-sharing agreements in this strategic sector. Watch for concrete deliverables from the India-France Year of Innovation initiative, which could include bilateral defence procurement announcements, joint research frameworks in artificial intelligence (given Macron's participation in the AI Impact Summit 2026), and expanded civil nuclear cooperation—each of which would signal the substance behind the elevated partnership declaration.

links.label: India-France Defence Partnership Deepens
links.preview: France has emerged as one of India's foremost defence partners with focus on co-design, co-development and co-production of defence platforms.

links.label: Economic Security in Indo-France Ties

links.label: Horizon 2047: Long-Term India-France Vision
links.preview: Strategic roadmap adopted in 2023 to guide bilateral cooperation through 2047, marking centenary of Indian independence and 50 years of strategic partnership.

links.label: Modi-Macron Reciprocal Diplomatic Engagement
links.preview: Macron's 2026 visit follows Modi's visit to France in February 2025, demonstrating sustained high-level bilateral momentum.

links.label: Artificial Intelligence Impact Summit 2026
links.preview: President Macron participated in the AI Impact Summit during his India visit, signalling technology cooperation as partnership cornerstone.
